title: StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, CronJobs, and Controller Selection · API Object Lens: K8s Controller Choice Defense
summary: A data intake system has interchangeable APIs, identity-bound workers, node agents, finite migrations, and scheduled reconciliation tasks requiring different controllers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s controller choice defense increment in controller selection and completion contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, CronJob, bare Pod, or custom controller selection from identity, completion, placement, update, and ownership evidence.
Design CronJob schedules, time zones, deadlines, concurrency policy, history, missed runs, clock assumptions, and idempotent work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Controller Choice Defense · Theory K8s Controller Choice Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory platform runs a durable database as a Deployment, a node log agent as one replica, and a finite migration as a long-running Pod. Choose Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, or CronJob from identity, placement, completion, concurrency, and retry contracts. Competency probe: Defend Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, CronJob, bare Pod, or custom controller selection from identity, completion, placement, update, and ownership evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Controller Choice Defense contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example platform runs a durable database as a Deployment, a node log agent as one replica, and a finite migration as a long-running Pod. Choose Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, or CronJob from identity, placement, completion, concurrency, and retry contracts. Competency probe: Defend Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, CronJob, bare Pod, or custom controller selection from identity, completion, placement, update, and ownership evidence. A defensible solution applies K8s Controller Choice Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to controller selection and completion contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-workload-controller-selection-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-workload-controller-selection-3
    artifact: controller-selection.yaml
    decision: worked k8s workload controller selection 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      stateless-api: Deployment
      identity-bound-worker: StatefulSet
      node-agent: DaemonSet
      one-time-migration: Job
      scheduled-reconcile: CronJob
      selection-proof: identity-completion-placement-update
    verify: kubectl get deploy,sts,ds,job,cronjob -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject Deployment-for-everything, exactly-once Job claims, CronJob clock certainty, or StatefulSet as automatic data replication.
    repair: Select from identity, completion, placement, update, storage, retry, idempotency, and failure evidence; then test controller-specific recovery.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Deployment is the safest default for every long-running or finite workload. This fails the K8s Controller Choice Defense contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
